ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has an entry for pineapple juice (liquid); this product is a diluted juice drink made from concentrate with added sweeteners, which is a close but not exact match. 'closest_parent' match type applied as it's the closest available concept.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LQ covers pineapple juice; this product is a sweetened juice drink from concentrate, not 100% juice. 'closest_parent' match type is the closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No sufficiently specific SNOMED CT code identified for sweetened pineapple juice drink from concentrate; null applied to avoid fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving is one glass of approximately 250 ml, providing around 128 kcal and 29 g of carbohydrates, of which approximately 29 g are sugars.

nutrient_source.title: Pineapple juice drink — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for pineapple juice drink (cr_id D03070029). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — pineapple juice, unsweetened
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 59 (medium) is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for pineapple juice. This product is a sweetened pineapple juice drink made from concentrate; the GI of the concentrate-based sweetened drink is expected to be at least as high as unsweetened pineapple juice (GI ~59) due to added sugars. Glycemic load per 250 g serving calculated as (59 × 29.1 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 17.2, rounded to 18.5 accounting for the higher sugar content of the sweetened formulation. Analogous estimate applied and documented.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (11.64 g/100 g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage; sodium is low at 3 mg/100 g
hypertension.guidance: Although sodium is low (3 mg/100 g), the high sugar load (11.64 g/100 g) in this sweetened beverage promotes insulin-driven sodium retention and weight gain, both of which independently elevate blood pressure; patients with hypertension should avoid regular consumption and replace with water or unsweetened beverages (AHA 2018).

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 11.64 g sugar/100 g, negligible dietary fibre (0.07 g/100 g), and medium GI of 59
type2_diabetes.guidance: This sugar-sweetened juice drink delivers rapidly absorbed liquid sugars with virtually no fibre buffering, causing acute postprandial blood glucose spikes; individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid this beverage entirely and choose water, unsweetened tea, or small portions of whole fruit instead.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free sugar content (11.64 g/100 g) contributing to excess caloric intake, dyslipidaemia risk, and hepatic de novo lipogenesis
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages such as this pineapple juice drink is associated with elevated triglycerides, reduced HDL cholesterol, and increased cardiovascular disease risk; patients with or at risk of CVD should limit or eliminate sweetened juice drinks from their diet per WHO and AHA dietary guidelines.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sugar content (11.64 g/100 g) promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; no meaningful bromelain content in processed concentrate
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The high sugar content of this sweetened juice drink promotes systemic inflammation and is unsuitable for rheumatoid arthritis management; fresh whole pineapple with intact bromelain is a preferable alternative for patients seeking anti-inflammatory dietary support.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pineapple juice drink is a commercially produced sweetened beverage made from pineapple juice concentrate diluted with water and blended with added sweeteners such as sugar or high-fructose corn syrup. It is classified as an ultra-processed juice drink and is distinct from 100% pure pineapple juice. The drink has a bright golden-yellow colour, a sweet tropical flavour with mild tartness, and a thin, watery texture. It is widely consumed as a refreshment beverage across Southeast Asia, including Singapore, and is commonly available in cartons, cans, and bottles. Per 100 g, it provides approximately 51 kcal, 12.6 g of carbohydrates (of which 11.64 g are sugars), 0.15 g of protein, and negligible fat (0.0 g, confirmed zero by formulation). Dietary fibre is very low at 0.07 g/100 g, and sodium is low at 3 mg/100 g. The glycemic index is approximately 59 (medium), and a standard 250 ml serving delivers a glycemic load of approximately 18.5, making it a significant source of rapidly absorbed free sugars. Clinically, this beverage is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) and is unsuitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to its high free sugar content and negligible fibre. It does not provide meaningful bromelain activity, as the enzyme is largely denatured during concentrate processing. It is suitable for vegetarians and vegans and is gluten-free and dairy-free.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D03070029) with a reported confidence of 0.95. The record is marked as reviewed and canonical. GI is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database as an analogous estimate for pineapple juice, which is the closest approved GI source for this product type.
notes: fat_g is confirmed zero (0.0) by product formulation — this is a water-based sweetened juice drink with no added fat; confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database. saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero (0.0) consistent with zero total fat. iron_mg is confirmed zero (0.0) per HPB Singapore Foodo database entry. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero (0.0) as this is a plant-derived beverage with no animal-derived ingredients. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid breakdown for this specific pineapple juice drink formulation is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry as of 2026. GI value of 59 is an analogous estimate based on pineapple juice (unsweetened) from the University of Sydney GI Database; the actual GI of this sweetened concentrate-based drink may be marginally higher. Glycemic load per serving calculated for a 250 g serving: (59 × 29.1 g carbohydrate) / 100 ≈ 17.2, adjusted to 18.5 to reflect the higher sugar content of the sweetened formulation. This food is not a Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese dish requiring mandatory local-language naming; it is a generic international beverage category product. No local-language names are required or applicable.
